description: The Detroit Partnership is an Italian American Mafia crime family based in Detroit, Michigan, founded around 1908 by Pietro Mirabile. It operates primarily in the Greater Detroit area, with additional territory in Ohio, Ontario, and Las Vegas. The family has been involved in racketeering, gambling, loan-sharking, labor racketeering, narcotics trafficking, and other illegal activities. Despite RICO prosecutions in the 1990s, the organization remains active in the 21st century.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: Primarily the Detroit metropolitan area, with additional territory throughout Michigan, Northwest Ohio and Southwestern Ontario...
country_links.context: The organization has territory in Southwestern Ontario, Canada.

country_links.evidence_quote: Utilizing its close links with the Sicilian Mafia , the Partnership became a major distributor of heroin in North America after World War II.
country_links.context: The Partnership utilized its close links with the Sicilian Mafia to become a major heroin distributor.

country_links.evidence_quote: Magaddino fled to Buffalo, New York , and Milazzo to Detroit
country_links.context: The organization operated in multiple U.S. cities including Detroit, Toledo, Las Vegas, and Buffalo.
